title: Lima / Cusco — first-ever international trip planning, with Dave
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir and "Dave" (likely david morris / david) planning their first international trip together — Aug 20 – Sep 4, 2021 to peru: lima → cusco → machu picchu → rainbow mountains.
  • Total budget breakdown: ~$1,333 — flight $528, internal flight to Cusco $45, housing 13×$20, food 13×$12, Machu Picchu $140, $200 misc.
  • Pitch letter co-signed by Dave + Yahir to invite other potential travelers — "Yahir and I will be going to Peru, and would love for all of you to join us on this remarkable trip."
  • Booked through American Airlines: explicit confirmation numbers Y087WJ8 (yg) and V187WJ2 (dave) — listed in the doc.
  • This is Yahir's origin trip — the first formal planning artifact for international travel in the vault, the seed of the non negotiables for traveling discipline.

What this is

The earliest dated travel-planning doc in the vault. Yahir is 18 in summer 2021 and the trip read as their "introduction to traveling." The doc is short (one-pager) but emotionally weighted — the all-nighter contemplating the decision is mentioned explicitly, and the writing voice is co-authored ("Yahir and I have come to a monumental conclusion that we want to go to PERU!!!!").

The pitch letter

The note that Yahir + Dave drafted to invite others reads like a recruitment pitch:

"We have spent countless days and an all-nighter tonight contemplating our decision, and we feel that visiting Machu Picchu and countless other breathtaking sights is going to be our introduction to traveling… our desired means of traveling is adventuring while being cost-efficient. We think inviting you guys on this endeavor would be something very memorable."

The framing — "adventuring while being cost-efficient" — is the seed of what later becomes Yahir's non negotiables for traveling (cheap, long-form, overland-or-equivalent, present-tense). Same DNA as 2024 07 02 Alt Summer Plan (India/Nepal/SEA) and 2023 08 14 Route from Cairo to Jordan (Egypt/Jordan).

The to-do list

A condensed checklist of what he had to figure out:

  • Reserve machu picchu visit
  • Tell parents
  • Advise work
  • Buy plane tickets (within next 6 days)
  • Buy "nice backblings" (gear)
  • Appropriate clothes
  • Reserve hostels/hotels
  • Book COVID tests (72 hrs before — this is summer 2021, covid pandemic era)
  • Cusco internal flight

The COVID-test requirement is a small artifact of the era — international travel in summer 2021 still required testing protocols.

Why it matters in the graph

This is the first chapter of Yahir's traveler self. The peru / machu picchu / lima / cusco entities all already exist in the vault from prior ingests, but this doc is the planning artifact that originated them — the ledger before the experience.

The travel discipline calibrated here (cost-conscious, partner-recruited, hostel-stay, multi-stop) becomes the template for every subsequent international trip. david morris / david / "Dave" is the recurring partner-from-earliest-days; the doc establishes him as the original co-conspirator.

Privacy

The doc lists American Airlines record-locator confirmation codes for both travelers (Y087WJ8 / V187WJ2) — these are 4+ years stale and the trip is long completed; not surfaced into this summary but flagged here. No active credentials.
